topics: Hrs. 30 Min. Prerequisites (if any) GCEST203 Course Type Lab Course Objectives

1. Provide practical experience in using AutoCAD software for 2D and 3D drawing 2. Equip students with the skills necessary to create and modify 2D drawing for industrial applications 3. Establish a solid foundation in 2D drawing to facilitate the transition to AutoCAD 3D 4. Prepare students to master similar graphics software
enhancing their versatility and adaptability in various design and engineering environments. Expt. No. Experiments 1 Introduction to Computer Aided Drafting

Assessment of pre-lab assignments or quizzes that test understanding of the upcoming experiment. ● Understanding of Theory
Evaluation based on students’ preparation and understanding of the theoretical background related to the experiments. Conduct of Experiments (7 Marks) ● Procedure and Execution
and following safety protocols. ● Skill Proficiency
and troubleshooting skills during the experiments. ● Teamwork
Collaboration and participation in group experiments. Lab Reports and Record Keeping (6 Marks) ● Quality of Reports
completeness and accuracy of lab reports. Proper documentation of experiments
data analysis and conclusions. ● Timely Submission
Adhering to deadlines for submitting lab reports/rough record and maintaining a well-organized fair record. Viva Voce (5 Marks) ● Oral Examination
results and underlying principles during a viva voce session. Final Marks Averaging

and record are the average of all the specified experiments in the syllabus. Evaluation Pattern for End Semester Examination (50 Marks) Procedure/Preliminary Work/Design/Algorithm (10 Marks) ● Procedure Understanding and Description
Clarity in explaining the procedure and understanding each step involved. ● Preliminary Work and Planning
Thoroughness in planning and organizing materials/equipment. ● Algorithm Development

Correctness and efficiency of the algorithm related to the experiment. ● Creativity and logic in algorithm or experimental design. Conduct of Experiment/Execution of Work/Programming (15 Marks) ● Setup and Execution
Proper setup and accurate execution of the experiment or programming task. Result with Valid Inference/Quality of Output (10 Marks) ● Accuracy of Results
Precision and correctness of the obtained results. ● Analysis and Interpretation
Validity of inferences drawn from the experiment or quality of program output. Viva Voce (10 Marks) ● Ability to explain the experiment
procedure results and answer related questions ● Proficiency in answering questions related to theoretical and practical aspects of the subject. Record (5 Marks) ● Completeness
and accuracy of the lab record submitted SEMESTER 4 POLYMER ENGINEERING SEMESTER S4 MATHEMATICS FOR PHYSICAL SCIENCE - 4
